buckets.payg: Personal/PAYG individual CONTEXT for spend (money out). NOT a deductibility claim — deductibility is decided later at year-end review.
buckets.company: Pty Ltd business expense (money out) — the company books of record.
buckets.property_rented: Expense (money out) on a currently-rented investment property.
buckets.property_vacant: Holding cost (money out) on a vacant/not-genuinely-available property.
buckets.income_business: Money IN (a credit) that is business revenue received by the Pty Ltd — sales, fees, invoices paid.
buckets.income_property: Money IN (a credit) that is rent received on an investment property (pair with the property).
buckets.income_personal: Money IN (a credit) that is personal income — salary/PAYG, interest, dividends received personally.
buckets.refund: Money IN (a credit) that REVERSES a prior expense (a refund/reversal) — nets against spend, it is NOT income.
buckets.asset: Money OUT (a debit) for a CAPITAL purchase — equipment/plant or a capital improvement that DEPRECIATES over its life. NOT an immediate deduction; it becomes a depreciating asset and is flagged for review. Use for clearly capital items (e.g. a laptop/computer, machinery, furniture, an appliance for a rental).

guidance: Categorise each line into exactly one bucket. A line marked money OUT (a debit / spend) takes an expense bucket (payg, company, property_*), or 'asset' if it is a clearly capital purchase that depreciates (large equipment, computers, machinery, furniture). A line marked money IN (a credit) takes an income bucket (income_business, income_property, income_personal) or 'refund' if it reverses an expense. Prefer 'unknown' over guessing — income and capital misclassification are costly. This is general information, not tax advice; never assert deductibility.

_asset_class_note: GENERAL-INFO effective-life defaults for auto-created depreciating assets. Approximations aligned with common ATO TR 2022/1 lives — a sensible starting point only. Auto-created assets still land needs_review for the user to confirm the class, effective life, method and business-use % with a registered tax agent.

claimability.general_info_note: Repairs that restore a rented property to working condition are generally deductible in the year paid (improvements are not).
A new appliance in a rented property is generally a depreciating asset (Div 40), claimed over its effective life — not immediately.
Holding costs are only deductible while the property is genuinely available for rent; vacant land holding costs are generally not deductible since 1 July 2019.
Car expenses on a novated lease are not deductible to the employee — the employer leases the car. The reportable fringe benefits amount (RFBA) is reportable.
Compulsory uniforms, union/professional fees and CPD self-education are commonly deductible for nurses. The initial cost of getting registered is not deductible.
Home-office running costs can be claimed at the 70c/hr fixed rate for 2024-25 and 2025-26; a work computer over $300 is depreciated rather than claimed immediately.

Rent you pay on your own home is generally a private expense and not deductible. Only a sole trader running a genuine 'place of business' from home can apportion occupancy costs — employees and remote workers generally cannot claim rent (only running costs).
Rent on business or commercial premises you lease is generally deductible. If the premises is partly private, only the business-use portion is claimable.
Commercial premises rent paid by your company is generally a deductible business expense. Apportion out any private use.

thresholds_by_fy._note: All dollar thresholds in CENTS. These change yearly and are policy-dependent — the depreciation engine + claimability brain read them per FY, never inline. Confirm against ATO before relying on them.
